name: Emily Cain, EMILY's List
subject: [Official Supporter Record] Have you helped elect women yet?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by EMILY's List [www
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c4d8c/24c69fd9/272425d26/1885a231/2952958737/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFES69N_digital_email_20180727_JulyEOMSupRec_inact&source=WQEFES69N_digital_email_20180727_JulyEOMSupRec_inact")
---

Peter,

This is it:

We're in the _last_ full quarter before Election Day.

But before we can get ready to defeat Republicans in the general election, we need to help all of our women who still have primary elections coming up in the next few months.

Your strongest support is needed right now to make sure all of our women can continue to win this year and on Election Day.

So I've taken a look at our supporter records to see where we all can pitch in, and here's what I have down for this email address:
**2018 Cycle Donor Status:** Not yet
**Suggested Action:[Donate $3 to help reach our $300,000 End-of-Month goal and help pro-choice Democratic women win this cycle.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c4d8c/24c69fd9/272425d26/1885a232/2952958737/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180727_email_JulyEOM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+if+consinConsGroup9225+Donate+again+else&utm_campaign=WQEFES69N_digital_email_20180727_JulyEOMSupRec_inact&source=WQEFES69N_digital_email_20180727_JulyEOMSupRec_inact")**
We've endorsed 93 women who still have to make it through their primary elections between now and the end of September.

I know what you're thinking: That's a _whole_ lot of elections. And it is.

But we've done it before: We've helped more than 100 candidates win their primary elections already!

And with the continued support of folks like you, we can keep going.

**Donate $3 to help reach our $300,000 End-of-Month goal and help our women win primary elections all summer long:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Hit-July-Goal](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c4d8c/24c69fd9/272425d26/1885a233/2952958737/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180727_email_JulyEOM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgHitJulyGoal&utm_campaign=WQEFES69N_digital_email_20180727_JulyEOMSupRec_inact&source=WQEFES69N_digital_email_20180727_JulyEOMSupRec_inact")**

Thanks -- and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c4d8c/24c69fd9/272425d26/1885a22c/2952958737/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180727_email_JulyEOM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFES69N_digital_email_20180727_JulyEOMSupRec_inact&source=WQEFES69N_digital_email_20180727_JulyEOMSupRec_inact")

\---
If you've donated recently, with a different email address, or offline, thank you! These records may not reflect those additional contributions, but don't worry, we have it recorded.

|
# EMILY's List is a community of over five million members that helps elect pro-choice Democratic women to office.

name: Team Morse
subject: 100 Days Until Election Day Event in Truckee!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jessica Morse for Congress, a grassroots campaign
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/a8SdruLmBt2FKuBwp8KvJWTkme_eZuQm1SYQucr9KvQ=/iirx4iCKV-bVRBeHcMoMslqLFRWndkNX5frykXdkYjQ=?refcode=email_ngp_fr_20180606_JM_gameon_all_logo&recurring=auto)
Peter,

This Sunday we will mark #100DaysUntilElectionDay by redoubling our efforts to call our neighbors and knock our district's doors. You don't want to miss out. Bring your friends and family to enjoy refreshments and listen to Jessica Morse speak on a live stream!

This is the time to get involved. This is the moment to step up and do your part. With only 100 days until the election, we need your help NOW before it's too late. Help keep up the momentum and join us this Sunday!

subject: He Actually Said It
body:
This week, Democrat Joe Donnelly said exactly what he's been doing for years, but rarely comes out and actually says: He told his national Democrat... |
---
| |
---
|
---
|
---
| **_He Actually Said It_** This week, Democrat Joe Donnelly said exactly what he's been doing for years, but rarely comes out and actually says: He told his national Democrat donors that during non-election years, he's a solid vote in the Democrat column...especially on the votes when Hoosiers really need him the most.

Just read what he said in an email this week to Democratic National Committee donors:

_“I intend to continue defending our Democratic values, but right now I am one of the most vulnerable senators up for re-election. Frankly this seat is one we cannot afford to lose. If we're going to have the chance to advance a commonsense Democratic agenda after November's election, we need to work together to hold this seat.”_

Yes, you read that right. Democrat Donnelly just admitted that if he can just recruit a few more deep-pocketed liberals to help him run ads trying to fool Hoosiers, he'll once again go to Washington to be an in-the-bag vote for all of the obstruction Democrats are trying to stir up.

Under the leadership of President Trump and Republicans in Congress, we're finally cutting red tape and seeing record economic growth. Earlier today, news broke that so many critics said would never happen. Thanks to President Trump's tax cuts (that Democrat Donnelly voted against), the U.S. economy is growing at a rate over four percent, which marks the [fastest growth in nearly four years!](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c897afdebe&e=1acd9bb3d6) This is huge news, and is a direct result of Republican leadership in Washington. Democrat Donnelly is telling liberals that he wants to stop that progress in its tracks.

But we're not going to let him get away with it, and U.S. Senate candidate**Mike Braun** is helping lead that charge!

For example, earlier this week when it looked like Democrat Donnelly was going to just follow Chuck Schumer's lead and not even _meet_ with President Trump's Supreme Court nominee Judge Brett Kavanaugh, Mike Braun put the pressure on Donnelly. Within days of laying down that pressure, Democrat Donnelly announced that he's finally put a meeting with Judge Kavanaugh on this schedule.

It's clear that if Democrat Donnelly had his way -- and if Hoosier like you weren't so engaged -- he'd be a vote in the Democrat column 100% of the time. But he knows we're watching, and that Mike Braun is going to keep him accountable, so instead he's on his guard.

Come November, let's elect Mike Braun to be our next United States Senator...someone we can trust to vote with Hoosiers, election year or not.

-Kyle

_**Photo:** Indiana Republican Party Chairman Kyle Hupfer spoke at the final Buck Garden Party to a crowd of revved-up Republicans in Kokomo last weekend!_
---
| |
---
| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
|
---
| **_It's a Team Sport_**
This week, **Governor Eric Holcomb** met with the state championship girls basketball team from Warren Central High School -- talking to them about teamwork and the Hoosiers who exemplify it through their work.

But when it comes to being a team player, **Governor Holcomb** is one of the absolute best.

This week, **Governor Holcomb** continued to build Indiana's critical relationship with Japan, meeting with another regional Japanese governor, and helped cut the ribbon on a new health clinic for Fiat Chrysler employees in Tipton. That's while his teammates helped officially launch a partnership this week with Walgreens, placing drug take-back stations in their stores statewide.

And, of course, that's while this week 94% of all Indiana public schools -- as well as many public charters and private schools -- applied for handheld metal detectors from the state to help keep Hoosier students safe.

Under**Governor Holcomb** 's team, it's been another great week for Indiana!
---
|
---
| | **_The Mike Braun Corner_**
---
|
---
| **_Keeping On The Pressure_**
U.S. Senate candidate**Mike Braun** is hard at work, meeting with Hoosiers (like, as pictured, at the Boone County Fair!) and keeping the pressure on Democrat Joe Donnelly...especially when it comes to putting another jurist on the Supreme Court who understands the original meaning of the U.S. Constitution!

When it looked like Democrat Donnelly wasn't even going to put a meeting on his calendar with Judge Brett Kavanaugh, President Trump's pick, **Mike Braun** put out a statement letting Hoosiers know:

_"It’s incredibly disappointing that Democrat Senator Donnelly and his Democrat colleagues are refusing to meet with President Trump’s highly qualified Supreme Court nominee, Judge Brett Kavanaugh. Senator Donnelly’s inability to even schedule a meeting with Judge Kavanaugh is another reminder that he puts the interests of the Democrat Party before Hoosiers and I urge Senator Donnelly to put partisanship aside and meet with him immediately.”_

And then after two weeks...and pressure from Mike Braun...Democrat Donnelly finally put a meeting with Judge Kavanaugh on his schedule. It's time for Democrat Donnelly to decide if he wants to stand with liberals in Washington, or with Hoosiers and President Trump's highly qualified-choice for the Supreme Court!

Let's continue to help **Mike Braun** keep Democrat Donnelly accountable for his actions, and send him packing this November!
---
|
---
| | **_National Week of Action_**
---
|
---
| **_Make a Difference This Weekend!_**
This weekend, get ready for the next National Week of Action!

From July 27-July 31, Hoosiers will be making calls, knocking on doors, and talking to their neighbors about **Mike Braun** and our entire 2018 statewide team! The voter contact we do now is critical to helping build the support we’ll need to deliver victories up and down the ballot in November.

**\-- > Sign-up [here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=99889eb4c8&e=1acd9bb3d6)**, and we’ll get you plugged in to make a difference in your community this weekend! Anything helps!
---
|
---
| | **_Apply for the Lugar Series!_**
---
|
---
| **_Applications Due Next Week!_**
Are you a female looking to get more involved in your community? Or do you know someone who is? There’s only four days left to apply to join the 2018-2019 class of the Richard G. Lugar Excellent in Public Service Series!

The Lugar Series is a distinguished leadership training course that prepares women for public service, with 20 women selected each year to join this eight-month program that concludes with a four-day trip to Washington, D.C.

Applications must be postmarked by August 1, so start completing your application [here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6961997dda&e=1acd9bb3d6) today!
---
|
---
| _**Upcoming Republican Events**_
[July 30](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e753a12b9b&e=1acd9bb3d6): Hancock County General Election Candidate Meeting
[August 1](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0d12a3f4f2&e=1acd9bb3d6): Jim Baird Barn Party
[August 9](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=10dcaacdeb&e=1acd9bb3d6): Boone County GOP Annual Golf Scramble
[August 10](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1d4d52d66a&e=1acd9bb3d6): Vanderburgh County Reagan Day
[August 11](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=89b258d21f&e=1acd9bb3d6): Harrison County Monthly Party Meeting
[August 17](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b6bf54b1d2&e=1acd9bb3d6): 27th Annual Newton County GOP Golf Outing
---
|
---
| _**News You Can Use**_ **Governor Eric Holcomb** [helps Indiana make connections around the world](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2424440c26&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch** [thanks those who care for Hoosiers with disabilities](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7a317a3e49&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Secretary of State Connie Lawson** [urges Hoosiers to maintain their businesses with INBiz](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=74c9dd7faa&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Treasurer Kelly Mitchell** [celebrates anniversary of INvestABLE Indiana savings plan launch](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8378ecd8b2&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Superintendent Jennifer McCormick **[talks about school safety with first responses and school officials](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f7882dccc2&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Senator Todd Young** [discusses solutions for the affordable housing crisis](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d031867bb8&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congresswoman Jackie Walorski** [votes to repeal the job-killing medical device tax](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7a343eb97d&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Jim Banks** [helps pass a bill to increase military spending](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fa0287aa74&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Todd Rokita** [attends the Buck Garden Party](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=702eefd8aa&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congresswoman Susan Brooks** [gives a radio interview talking about her work in Congress](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f8a3822f82&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Luke Messer** [passes his bill to extend the Lewis and Clark National Historic Trail](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c9a87bda41&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Larry Bucshon** [votes to give Americans more affordable health choices](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=86d867c457&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Trey Hollingsworth** [is profiled in Forbes](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f80c97f178&e=1acd9bb3d6)
